regions.name: North Africa
regions.subtitle: The Maghreb Ceremony
regions.description: In Morocco, Tunisia, and Algeria, tea is not a beverage — it is an act of hospitality. Poured from great height into ornate glasses, Maghreb mint tea is a symbol of friendship, negotiation, and welcome.

culturalPairing.ceremony: The three glasses tradition — the first is gentle as life, the second strong as love, the third bitter as death.
culturalPairing.music: Gnawa rhythms and Andalusian classical melodies

culturalPairing.ceremony: Brewed in a small pot over coals, served in three rounds of increasing sweetness.
culturalPairing.music: Tinariwen — Desert blues and Tuareg guitar
culturalPairing.literature: Ibrahim al-Koni — The Bleeding of the Stone

teas.name: Moroccan Rosewater Tisane
teas.type: Herbal Infusion — Rose Petals, Orange Blossom, Verbena

culturalPairing.ceremony: Served after meals in the riads of Marrakech — a gentle, floral close to the evening.
culturalPairing.music: Andalusian oud — the melodies of the medina at dusk
culturalPairing.literature: Tahar Ben Jelloun — The Sacred Night

regions.name: East Asia
regions.subtitle: The Way of Tea
regions.description: From Japan's meditative tea ceremonies to China's ancient Gongfu tradition and Taiwan's high-mountain oolongs, East Asian tea culture is a philosophy — a practice of presence, respect, and craft.

culturalPairing.ceremony: Once called 'the people's tea,' genmaicha was born from adding toasted rice to stretch precious tea leaves — warmth and humility in a cup.
culturalPairing.music: Koto — the 13-string plucked instrument of Japanese court music

culturalPairing.ceremony: Gongfu Cha — small clay pots, multiple short steeps, each pour revealing new layers of the leaf's character.
culturalPairing.music: Erhu — the two-string fiddle that sings like a human voice
culturalPairing.literature: Lin Yutang — The Importance of Living

culturalPairing.ceremony: Roasted over charcoal in porcelain pots, hojicha is the evening tea of Japan — warm, nutty, and naturally low in caffeine. A cup of calm.
culturalPairing.literature: Kakuzo Okakura — The Book of Tea

regions.name: South America
regions.subtitle: The Circle of Yerba
regions.description: In Argentina, Uruguay, and southern Brazil, sharing mate from a single gourd is a tradition of equality and trust — a circle where everyone drinks from the same vessel.

culturalPairing.ceremony: The cebador prepares and serves. Saying 'gracias' means you're done. The gourd passes left.
culturalPairing.music: Chamame folk music of the Litoral region

culturalPairing.ceremony: Shared among gauchos on the pampas, prepared fresh with a cuia and bomba.
culturalPairing.music: Milonga — the folk music of the southern plains
culturalPairing.literature: Érico Veríssimo — O Tempo e o Vento

teas.name: Mate Cocido de Hierbas
teas.type: Herbal Infusion — Mint, Lemon Verbena, Chamomile

culturalPairing.ceremony: An evening version of the mate circle — herbal, gentle, and perfect for those who want the warmth without the buzz.
culturalPairing.music: Zamba — the slow, swaying dance music of the Argentine north

regions.name: South Asia
regions.subtitle: Chai as Communion
regions.description: From the bustling chaiwallahs of Mumbai to the misty plantations of Darjeeling, tea in South Asia is woven into the rhythm of daily life — every street corner, every conversation.

culturalPairing.ceremony: Simmered slowly with cardamom, ginger, cinnamon, and cloves. Served in small clay cups — kulhads.
culturalPairing.music: Ravi Shankar — Sitar ragas at dusk
culturalPairing.literature: Arundhati Roy — The God of Small Things

culturalPairing.ceremony: Known as the 'Champagne of Teas,' best enjoyed without milk to appreciate its muscatel notes.
culturalPairing.music: Sarod melodies — the reflective tones of the hills
culturalPairing.literature: Ruskin Bond — A Town Called Dehra

teas.name: Kashmiri Kahwa
teas.type: Herbal Infusion — Saffron, Cinnamon, Cardamom, Almonds

culturalPairing.ceremony: Brewed in a traditional samovar, kahwa is the golden welcome of the Kashmir Valley — saffron threads floating in warmth.
culturalPairing.music: Santoor — the hammered dulcimer of the Kashmir Valley
culturalPairing.literature: Agha Shahid Ali — The Country Without a Post Office
